key_insight: A hybrid material point method can accurately capture large bending deformations of slender structures by resolving the coupled interaction between cross-sectional distortion and lengthwise flexure.
engineering_relevance: This provides a practical simulation framework for predicting bending behavior in flexible tubes, tape springs, soft robots, and deployable structures where contact and instability are difficult to model.
method: Hybrid-MPM couples Lagrangian finite elements with Eulerian finite differences.
Simulates experimental three-point bending tests with complex contact.
Applied to elastic tubes and tape springs exhibiting Brazier-type coupling.
evidence_signal: The simulations were validated against desktop experiments and classical theory, showing excellent agreement in bending response and deformation patterns.
watch_item: Accuracy may depend on careful contact handling and calibration for highly nonlinear large-deformation cases.
paper_title: Numerical and experimental framework for bending elasticity of highly flexible slender structures
